info.description: REST API for the Content Control Center application.
info.title: Content Control Center API

get.description:
Returns the set of Zernio platform identifiers Ogen is
permitted to auto-publish to. Empty by default.

put.description:
Replaces the auto-publish allowlist with the supplied
set of Zernio platform identifiers. Replace-not-append:
any platform absent from the body is removed. An empty
array clears the allowlist.

get.description: Returns all campaign types with their phases ordered by name.

post.description: Creates a new user-defined campaign type without phases.

get.description: Returns a single campaign type with its phases by ID.

put.description: Updates name, label, and description of a user-created campaign type. System types cannot be modified.

delete.description: Deletes a user-created campaign type and all its phases. System types cannot be deleted.

post.description: Creates a new user-defined campaign type by deep-copying an existing type and all its phases.

parameters.description: Name and label for the clone
parameters.name: body

post.description: Adds a new phase to a campaign type.

put.description: Updates a phase within a campaign type.

delete.description: Deletes a phase from a campaign type.

get.description: Returns all campaigns ordered by creation date.

post.description: Creates a new campaign. The created_by field is set from the authenticated session.

get.description: Returns all posts for the given campaign ID, with campaign, platform, and assets hydrated.
Returns a single campaign by Sqid.

put.description: Replaces all mutable fields of an existing campaign.

post.description:
Generates a campaign brief (description, target persona, key messages, tone guidelines)
from the campaign's title and type, streaming progress via Server-Sent Events.
Per-field "*_delta" events preview each value as it is written; a final "complete"
event carries the full EnrichBriefResponse and is the signal that the brief is ready.
On failure an "error" event carries {"message":"<text>","code":<http_code>}.
The brief is returned as a suggestion only — it is not persisted to the campaign.

200.description: SSE stream: step / *_delta / complete / error events

post.description:
Calls the AI content plan flow and streams progress via Server-Sent Events.
Each step emits an SSE event of type "step" with payload {"step":"<name>","status":"done"}.
On success a final "complete" event carries the full ContentPlanResponse payload.
On failure an "error" event carries {"message":"<text>","code":<http_code>}.

200.description: SSE stream: step / complete / error events

get.description: Returns all content bank assets ordered by creation date.

post.description: Creates a new content bank asset. The created_by field is set from the authenticated session.

Accepts one or more `.md` (max 10 MB) or `.pdf` (max 50 MB) files. Markdown files are converted to BlockNote JSON synchronously; PDFs are uploaded to object storage and processed asynchronously (text extraction, page-aware chunking, embedding, thumbnail). Files are processed independently — one failure does not block others.

get.description: Returns a single content bank asset by Sqid.

put.description: Updates title and content of an existing asset.

delete.description: Deletes a content bank asset by Sqid.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user derived from the active session cookie.

Subscribes the authenticated user to a long-lived
Server-Sent Events stream filtered by topic patterns.

Topic filters are passed via the `topics` query param as a
comma-separated list. Supported forms per filter:
- "all"               — receive every event the user is
authorised to see.
- "kind:id"           — exact match (e.g. "entity:post:Xq").
- "kind:*"            — prefix wildcard (e.g. "job:*").

Each event is emitted as one SSE frame:
id: <event-id>
event: <type>
data: <json>

A heartbeat comment (`: ping`) is sent every 20 seconds.
Delivery is at-most-once; the server holds no event log.
Subscribers are disconnected if their per-connection buffer
overflows (the client should reconnect and reconcile via REST).

The `Last-Event-ID` request header is currently accepted
and ignored — reserved for future replay support.

Returns the health status of the service, database
connectivity, and per-secret resolvability. A secret
that is present but undecryptable downgrades the
overall status to "degraded" without failing the
endpoint — boot stayed up under the same rule.

post.description: Uploads an image file to object storage and returns its public URL.

get.description:
Returns the local view of accounts attached via the
Zernio profile, plus the last sync timestamp + status.
Reads from SQLite — does not call Zernio.

post.description:
Returns a one-shot connect URL that the user opens in
a browser to authorize a social account on the chosen
platform. The URL contains a short-lived token.

get.description:
Public endpoint suitable for inclusion in monitoring
dashboards. Reports whether the integration is enabled,
its state (disabled / degraded / ok), the bootstrapped
profile ID, and the most recent sync result.

Deprecated: prefer GET /api/platforms, which surfaces the
same publisher information (and connection state) inline
per platform. This endpoint stays for clients that
haven't migrated yet and will be removed in a later
cycle.

Returns the Phase 1 allowlist with each platform's
supportedPostTypes derived from the local platforms
table.

post.description:
Triggers a fresh Zernio profile bootstrap for operational
recovery. Idempotent: a successful repair leaves the
integration in the "ok" state.

Asks the background worker to run a sync at its
earliest opportunity. Returns 202 with the previous
last_sync_at value so callers can poll for progress.

get.description:
Returns all platforms ordered by creation date. Each
platform carries a `publishers` array describing
whether it's connected via any of the configured
publisher backends (e.g. Zernio) and which post
types each publisher can post in.

post.description: Creates a new platform.

get.description:
Returns a single platform with the same `publishers`
enrichment as the list endpoint.

put.description: Replaces all mutable fields of an existing platform.

get.description:
Returns each post-type slug supported by the platform
with the structural rules enforced by the publish gate
(CON-74) — required content, allowed attachment kinds,
and min/max attachment counts. `max_attachments` is
resolved against the platform's per-kind cap; `null`
means unbounded. Slugs that are whitelist-only carry
`rule: null` and `whitelist_only: true`.
get.summary: List per-post-type rules for a platform

get.description:
Returns log entries filtered by any combination of
`post_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `since`/`until`
(RFC3339), and `limit`. Default order is newest first.
Returns all posts ordered by creation date, with campaign, platform, and assets hydrated.

post.description:
Creates a new post. The created_by field is set from the authenticated session.
The `content` field is a Markdown string; the frontend renders it via BlockNote.
`platform_id` and `platform_post_type` are required only when `status` is not `draft`;
drafts can be saved without a platform chosen up front.

get.description: Returns a single post by Sqid with campaign, platform, and assets hydrated.

put.description:
Replaces all mutable fields of an existing post.
The `content` field is a Markdown string; the frontend renders it via BlockNote.
`platform_id` and `platform_post_type` are required only when the new `status` is
not `draft`; transitioning a draft to `ready_for_publish` (or beyond) without a
platform chosen returns 400.

post.description:
Runs the Post quality assessment agent (CON-85) and streams
Server-Sent Events: a "step" event per flow stage, then a
final "complete" event carrying the persisted evaluation
(four 0-10 dimension scores with rationale, weakness, and
span-anchored suggestions, plus the backend-computed overall).

get.description:
Returns the most recent persisted quality evaluation for a
post (CON-85/CON-92) without invoking the model. The frontend
reads this to render an existing assessment and only triggers
POST /assess when the post has changed since it was scored.

post.description:
Sends an instruction to the AI assistant and streams progress via Server-Sent Events.
Events: "explanation_delta" and "content_delta" carry {"delta":"..."} fragments (Markdown)
as the model generates the explanation and updated content. "tool_call" and "tool_result"
signal asset-retrieval tool invocations. "complete" carries the final PostAssistantResponse
whose updatedContent is a Markdown string.
"error" carries {"message":"...","code":<http_code>}.

200.description: SSE stream: delta / tool_call / tool_result / complete / error events

post.description:
Enqueues a Backlite cancel_zernio_job task. The Post
remains in `Scheduled` until Zernio confirms the
cancellation; on confirmation it transitions to the
requested target (`ready_for_publish` or `draft`).
If Zernio reports the job has already been published
(race), the cancellation is a no-op and the next poll
cycle lands `Published` per the normal success path
(CON-69 §9).

Duplicates a post as a new draft in the same campaign and phase, copying
title, content, media/attachments, used assets, CTA, and audience notes.
Attachments are deep-copied in object storage so the clone is independent
of its source. Pass target_platform_id/target_post_type to retarget the
clone (verbatim content — the assistant path adapts content for you).

get.description: Returns the most recent assistant conversation messages for a post.
Returns all version snapshots for a post.

post.description: Manually creates a version snapshot of the current post content.

get.description:
Returns the post's attachments ordered by position, with a
soft pre-check (`platform_validation`) per CON-73 §2.4 that
surfaces any rule failure for the post's current target
platform. Each attachment carries a short-lived presigned
GET URL when object storage is configured.

post.description:
Accepts a single image (CON-73) or PDF (CON-75) file via
multipart/form-data under the field `file`. The file is
decoded server-side to validate the MIME — JPEG/PNG/WebP/GIF
for images, application/pdf for PDFs — then streamed to
object storage. Hard caps: 50 MB images, 100 MB PDFs.
PDF uploads also render a first-page PNG thumbnail
(best-effort; failures do not abort the upload).
Per-platform caps are surfaced as soft warnings in the
response.

409.description: post is in a terminal publishing state

get.description:
Returns metadata for a single attachment plus its soft
validation block and a short-lived presigned GET URL.

delete.description:
Removes an attachment row and its S3 object(s). For PDF
attachments this includes the rendered thumbnail. If any
S3 delete fails, the metadata row is retained and 502 is
returned so the caller can retry (CON-73 §2.7).

patch.description:
Updates the `position` of an attachment. Last-write-wins;
no optimistic concurrency token (CON-73 §6 MVP decision).

get.description:
Returns the audit log for a single post in chronological
order (oldest first). Default cap 500 entries; pass
`?limit=N` to override.
get.summary: List Post Log entries for a post

get.description:
Returns metadata for every persisted secret. Never
returns ciphertext, nonces, wrapped DEKs, or plaintext.

Returns metadata for a single secret. 404 if absent;
the plaintext is never returned.

put.description:
Creates or replaces a secret. The plaintext is never
echoed back; the response carries metadata only.

delete.description:
Removes a secret. Features depending on the deleted
key degrade on next call (no boot failure). 404 if
the name is absent.

post.description: Authenticates a user by email and password, sets a session cookie, and returns the created session.

delete.description: Invalidates the current session and clears the session cookie.

get.description: Returns all key-value settings.
Returns a single setting by key. Authentication is not required when setup_complete is false, allowing the frontend to check setup status before any user exists.

put.description: Creates or updates a setting by key.

get.description: Returns all tags ordered by creation date.

post.description: Creates a new tag. The created_by field is set from the authenticated session.

get.description: Returns a single tag by Sqid.

put.description: Updates name and color of an existing tag.

get.description: Returns all users ordered by creation date.

post.description: Creates a new user. Open (no auth required) while setup_complete=false; requires authentication once setup is complete.

get.description: Returns a single user by Sqid.

put.description: Updates name and/or email of an existing user.

presigned_url.description:
PresignedURL and ThumbnailURL are hydrated by the handler at
response time; they are not stored in the database.

target.description:
Target is the status the user wants the post moved to once
Zernio confirms the cancellation. Defaults to "ready_for_publish"
when omitted (CON-69 §9 — ReadyForPublish is the most common
"cancel and edit" landing state).

platform_id.description:
PlatformID + PlatformPostType are required only when status is not
"draft" — see requirePlatformIfNotDraft below. Drafts can be saved
before the user has picked a platform (CON-60).

password.description: Password is optional on update; when provided it must be at least 8 characters.

thumbnail_url.description:
ThumbnailURL is a transient public URL rendered from ThumbnailS3Key by
the handler layer before serialization. Not persisted.

weight.description:
Weight is the fraction (0-1) this dimension contributed to the
overall, taken from the Type weight profile. Contribution is the
weighted percentage points it added to overall_pct
(Weight * Score/10 * 100). Both are backend-computed.

campaign.description: Hydrated relations — not stored in the database.

cloned_from_post_id.description:
ClonedFromPostID links a clone back to the Post it was duplicated
from (CON-59). Nil for posts created directly. `nullzero` sends
NULL (not "") so the lineage is a clean "has a source / does not".

platform_id.description:
platform_id and platform_post_type are nullable so draft posts can
exist without a platform chosen up front (CON-60). They become
required when the post moves out of "draft" — enforced by the
posts handler, not by the schema.

`nullzero` makes bun send NULL (not "") for empty values, which is
required for platform_id because the FK to platforms can't match
an empty string. We use it on platform_post_type too for symmetry.

zernio_post_id.description:
Zernio integration fields (CON-69 §6/§7).
ZernioPostID is the id Zernio assigns when a Post is submitted;
the UNIQUE index in the migration prevents accidental double-submit.
ZernioStatus mirrors Zernio's enum verbatim so the polling task
can short-circuit on already-handled terminal states.
PublishedResults carries Zernio's per-platform outcomes (URLs,
platform IDs) once the post resolves.
FailureReason distinguishes Zernio-reported failure from
reconciliation timeout — see CON-69 §8.

input_hash.description:
InputHash fingerprints the assessment inputs (rendered prompt + model)
so the assess flow skips re-running the model when nothing the model
sees has changed (CON-92).
